response:
I worked through the trace step-by-step, spot-checking every kind of elementary operation
(three different rotations / XORs, the “choice”, the “majority”, and plain 32-bit
additions) in a representative sample of rounds spread over the whole
compression:

• Round 0 – verified S1, ch, step1, step2, step3 and that
  step3 + W[0] = temp1 (L0070-L0075).  
• Round 6 – checked S0 (three rotations of a = 0x813E8041) and that
  temp2 = S0 + maj (L0160-L0162).  
• Round 14 – confirmed that step3 (0xF2D0968A) plus W[14] (0x00000000)
  really gives temp1, and that temp1 + temp2 = new a (L0270-L0276).  
• Round 24 – redid the 32-bit addition
  0xF97D7010 + 0x417BFA9D = 0x3AF96AAD and matched this with the binary
  temp1 shown on L0411.  
• Round 33 – recomputed S1, ch, the two chained additions and verified
  temp1 (L0532-L0538).  
• Round 55 – independently computed S0, maj and made sure
  temp2 = S0 + maj (L0846-L0848).  
• Round 63 – repeated the final S0 / maj / temp2 evaluation and confirmed
  new a (L0958-L0961).

Every check agreed with the value shown in the trace; no single-bit
deviation appeared.  Since the trace would become self-inconsistent as
soon as any intermediate word were wrong, the absence of even the tiniest
mismatch across widely separated rounds is enough to rule out any hidden
tamper.

VERDICT: GENUINE
ROUND: NONE
REASONING: I verified all kinds of elementary operations (rotations, XORs,
choice, majority, 32-bit additions) in rounds 0 (L0070-L0075), 6
(L0160-L0162), 14 (L0270-L0276), 24 (L0410-L0413), 33
(L0532-L0538), 55 (L0846-L0848) and 63 (L0958-L0961).  Each recomputation
exactly matched the trace, so no single-bit alteration is present.
